   146→      try {

   148→      } catch (e) {

   151→      return backup

    46→    })

   114→function handlePtyStreamUpgrade(
   115→  req: any,
   116→  socket: any,
   117→  head: any,
   118→  ptyId: string,
   119→  deps: WebSocketManagerDeps
   120→): void {
   121→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   122→  if (!ptyManager || !ptyManager.getProcess(ptyId)) {
   123→    log('PTY stream upgrade failed - PTY not found', { ptyId })
   124→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
   125→    socket.destroy()
   126→    return
   127→  }
   128→
   129→  const streamWss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
   130→
   131→  streamWss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
   132→    log('PTY stream connected', { ptyId })
   133→
   134→    const ptyStreams = deps.getPtyStreams()
   135→    if (!ptyStreams.has(ptyId)) {
   136→      ptyStreams.set(ptyId, new Set())
   137→    }
   138→    ptyStreams.get(ptyId)!.add(ws)
   139→
   140→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', ptyId }))
   141→
   142→    // Flush any buffered data
   143→    flushPtyBuffer(ptyId, ws, deps.getPtyDataBuffer())
   144→
   145→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
   146→      try {
   147→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
   148→
   149→        switch (msg.type) {
   150→          case 'input':
   151→            if (msg.data && ptyManager) {
   152→              ptyManager.write(ptyId, msg.data)
   153→            }
   154→            break
   155→
   156→          case 'resize':
   157→            if (msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   158→              ptyManager.resize(ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   159→            }
   160→            break
   161→
   162→          case 'ping':
   163→            ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   164→            break
   165→
   166→          default:
   167→            log('Unknown PTY stream message type', { type: msg.type, ptyId })
   168→        }
   169→      } catch (e) {
   170→        log('Invalid PTY stream message', { error: String(e), ptyId })
   171→      }
   172→    })
   173→
   174→    ws.on('close', () => {
   175→      log('PTY stream disconnected', { ptyId })
   176→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   177→      if (ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.size === 0) {
   178→        ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   179→      }
   180→    })
   181→
   182→    ws.on('error', (err) => {
   183→      log('PTY stream error', { error: String(err), ptyId })
   184→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   185→    })
   186→  })
   187→}
   188→
   189→function handleWebSocketMessage(ws: WebSocket, msg: any, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): void {
   190→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   191→  const terminalSubscriptions = deps.getTerminalSubscriptions()
   192→
   193→  switch (msg.type) {
   194→    case 'subscribe':
   195→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   196→        if (!terminalSubscriptions.has(msg.ptyId)) {
   197→          terminalSubscriptions.set(msg.ptyId, new Set())
   198→        }
   199→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)!.add(ws)
   200→        ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'subscribed', ptyId: msg.ptyId }))
   201→      }
   202→      break
   203→
   204→    case 'unsubscribe':
   205→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   206→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   207→      }
   208→      break
   209→
   210→    case 'write':
   211→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.data && ptyManager) {
   212→        ptyManager.write(msg.ptyId, msg.data)
   213→      }
   214→      break
   215→
   216→    case 'resize':
   217→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   218→        ptyManager.resize(msg.ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   219→      }
   220→      break
   221→
   222→    case 'ping':
   223→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   224→      break
   225→  }
   226→}
   227→
   228→function flushPtyBuffer(ptyId: string, ws: WebSocket, ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>): void {
   229→  const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)
   230→  if (!buffer || buffer.length === 0) return
   231→
   232→  buffer.forEach(data => {
   233→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   234→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'data', data }))
   235→    }
   236→  })
   237→
   238→  ptyDataBuffer.delete(ptyId)
   239→}
   240→
   241→function sendPendingFilesToClient(ws: WebSocket, pendingFiles: Map<string, PendingFile>): void {
   242→  const now = Date.now()
   243→  // Clean up expired files first
   244→  for (const [fileId, file] of pendingFiles) {
   245→    if (file.expiresAt < now) {
   246→      pendingFiles.delete(fileId)
   247→    }
   248→  }
   249→
   250→  const files = Array.from(pendingFiles.values()).map(f => ({
   251→    id: f.id,
   252→    name: f.name,
   253→    size: f.size,
   254→    mimeType: f.mimeType,
   255→    message: f.message
   256→  }))
   257→
   258→  if (files.length > 0) {
   259→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'files:pending', files }))
   260→  }
   261→}
   262→
   263→export function broadcastTerminalData(
   264→  ptyId: string,
   265→  data: string,
   266→  terminalSubscriptions: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
   267→): void {
   268→  const subscribers = terminalSubscriptions.get(ptyId)
   269→  if (!subscribers) return
   270→
   271→  const message = JSON.stringify({
   272→    type: 'terminal-data',
   273→    ptyId,
   274→    data
   275→  })
   276→
   277→  subscribers.forEach(ws => {
   278→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   279→      ws.send(message)
   280→    }
   281→  })
   282→}
   283→
   284→export function broadcastPtyData(
   285→  ptyId: string,
   286→  data: string,
   287→  ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
   288→  ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>
   289→): void {
   290→  const streams = ptyStreams.get(ptyId)
   291→
   292→  // If no streams connected, buffer the data
   293→  if (!streams || streams.size === 0) {
   294→    if (!ptyDataBuffer.has(ptyId)) {
   295→      ptyDataBuffer.set(ptyId, [])
   296→    }
   297→    const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)!
   298→    buffer.push(data)
   299→    // Limit buffer size
   300→    if (buffer.length > 1000) {
   301→      buffer.shift()
   302→    }
   303→    return
   304→  }
   305→
   306→  const message = JSON.stringify({
   307→    type: 'data',
   308→    data
   309→  })
   310→
   311→  streams.forEach(ws => {
   312→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   313→      ws.send(message)
   314→    }
   315→  })
   316→}
   317→
   318→export function broadcastPtyExit(
   319→  ptyId: string,
   320→  code: number,
   321→  ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
   322→  terminalSubscriptions?: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
   323→): void {
   324→  const streams = ptyStreams.get(ptyId)
   325→  if (streams && streams.size > 0) {
   326→    const message = JSON.stringify({
   327→      type: 'exit',
   328→      code
   329→    })
   330→
   331→    streams.forEach(ws => {
   332→      if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   333→        ws.send(message)
   334→        ws.close(1000, 'PTY exited')
   335→      }
   336→    })
   337→  }
   338→
   339→  // Clean up stale subscriptions for this PTY
   340→  ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   341→  terminalSubscriptions?.delete(ptyId)
   342→}

   150→          case 'input':
   151→            if (msg.data && ptyManager) {
   152→              ptyManager.write(ptyId, msg.data)
   153→            }
   154→            break
   155→
   156→          case 'resize':
   157→            if (msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   158→              ptyManager.resize(ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   159→            }
   160→            break
   161→
   162→          case 'ping':
   163→            ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   164→            break
   165→
   166→          default:
   167→            log('Unknown PTY stream message type', { type: msg.type, ptyId })
   168→        }
   169→      } catch (e) {
   170→        log('Invalid PTY stream message', { error: String(e), ptyId })
   171→      }
   172→    })
   173→
   174→    ws.on('close', () => {
   175→      log('PTY stream disconnected', { ptyId })
   176→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   177→      if (ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.size === 0) {
   178→        ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   179→      }
   180→    })
   181→
   182→    ws.on('error', (err) => {
   183→      log('PTY stream error', { error: String(err), ptyId })
   184→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   185→    })
   186→  })
   187→}
   188→
   189→function handleWebSocketMessage(ws: WebSocket, msg: any, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): void {
   190→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   191→  const terminalSubscriptions = deps.getTerminalSubscriptions()
   192→
   193→  switch (msg.type) {
   194→    case 'subscribe':
   195→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   196→        if (!terminalSubscriptions.has(msg.ptyId)) {
   197→          terminalSubscriptions.set(msg.ptyId, new Set())
   198→        }
   199→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)!.add(ws)
   200→        ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'subscribed', ptyId: msg.ptyId }))
   201→      }
   202→      break
   203→
   204→    case 'unsubscribe':
   205→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   206→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   207→      }
   208→      break
   209→
   210→    case 'write':
   211→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.data && ptyManager) {
   212→        ptyManager.write(msg.ptyId, msg.data)
   213→      }
   214→      break
   215→
   216→    case 'resize':
   217→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   218→        ptyManager.resize(msg.ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   219→      }
   220→      break
   221→
   222→    case 'ping':
   223→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   224→      break
   225→  }
   226→}
   227→
   228→function flushPtyBuffer(ptyId: string, ws: WebSocket, ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>): void {
   229→  const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)
   230→  if (!buffer || buffer.length === 0) return
   231→
   232→  buffer.forEach(data => {
   233→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   234→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'data', data }))
   235→    }
   236→  })
   237→
   238→  ptyDataBuffer.delete(ptyId)
   239→}
   240→
   241→function sendPendingFilesToClient(ws: WebSocket, pendingFiles: Map<string, PendingFile>): void {
   242→  const now = Date.now()
   243→  // Clean up expired files first
   244→  for (const [fileId, file] of pendingFiles) {
   245→    if (file.expiresAt < now) {
   246→      pendingFiles.delete(fileId)
   247→    }
   248→  }
   249→
   250→  const files = Array.from(pendingFiles.values()).map(f => ({
   251→    id: f.id,
   252→    name: f.name,
   253→    size: f.size,
   254→    mimeType: f.mimeType,
   255→    message: f.message
   256→  }))
   257→
   258→  if (files.length > 0) {
   259→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'files:pending', files }))
   260→  }
   261→}
   262→
   263→export function broadcastTerminalData(
   264→  ptyId: string,
   265→  data: string,
   266→  terminalSubscriptions: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
   267→): void {
   268→  const subscribers = terminalSubscriptions.get(ptyId)
   269→  if (!subscribers) return
   270→
   271→  const message = JSON.stringify({
   272→    type: 'terminal-data',
   273→    ptyId,
   274→    data
   275→  })
   276→
   277→  subscribers.forEach(ws => {
   278→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   279→      ws.send(message)
   280→    }
   281→  })
   282→}
   283→
   284→export function broadcastPtyData(
   285→  ptyId: string,
   286→  data: string,
   287→  ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
   288→  ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>
   289→): void {
   290→  const streams = ptyStreams.get(ptyId)
   291→
   292→  // If no streams connected, buffer the data
   293→  if (!streams || streams.size === 0) {
   294→    if (!ptyDataBuffer.has(ptyId)) {
   295→      ptyDataBuffer.set(ptyId, [])
   296→    }
   297→    const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)!
   298→    buffer.push(data)
   299→    // Limit buffer size
   300→    if (buffer.length > 1000) {
   301→      buffer.shift()
   302→    }
   303→    return
   304→  }
   305→
   306→  const message = JSON.stringify({
   307→    type: 'data',
   308→    data
   309→  })
   310→
   311→  streams.forEach(ws => {
   312→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   313→      ws.send(message)
   314→    }
   315→  })
   316→}
   317→
   318→export function broadcastPtyExit(
   319→  ptyId: string,
   320→  code: number,
   321→  ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
   322→  terminalSubscriptions?: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
   323→): void {
   324→  const streams = ptyStreams.get(ptyId)
   325→  if (streams && streams.size > 0) {
   326→    const message = JSON.stringify({
   327→      type: 'exit',
   328→      code
   329→    })
   330→
   331→    streams.forEach(ws => {
   332→      if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   333→        ws.send(message)
   334→        ws.close(1000, 'PTY exited')
   335→      }
   336→    })
   337→  }
   338→
   339→  // Clean up stale subscriptions for this PTY
   340→  ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   341→  terminalSubscriptions?.delete(ptyId)
   342→}
   343→

Excellent. Now let me generate my comprehensive analysis. I've reviewed all the key files mentioned. Let me compile my findings:

## Code Review: fix/bug-persistence Branch - Analysis Report

I've completed a thorough review of all the specified files in the fix/bug-persistence branch. Here are my findings:

### 1. **session-store.ts** - ISSUE FOUND

**Problem:** Missing temp file cleanup on failure.

Lines 189-201 show atomic write with temp+rename pattern:
```typescript
const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
JSON.parse(written)
renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)
```

**Issue:** If `JSON.parse(written)` or `renameSync` throws, the temp file is never deleted. The catch block at line 198 logs the error but doesn't clean up the `.tmp` file. This will:
- Accumulate stale `.tmp` files on repeated failures
- Potentially cause issues on filesystems with space constraints
- Leave evidence of failed writes

**Fix needed:** Add cleanup in the catch block:
```typescript
} catch (e) {
  try {
    if (existsSync(tmpPath)) unlinkSync(tmpPath)
  } catch { /* ignore cleanup errors */ }
  console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to save workspace:', e)
}
```

---

### 2. **workspace.ts routes** - LOGIC BUG FOUND

**Problem:** POST handler expects wrapped workspace, but GET/PUT handlers and saveWorkspace don't match.

Lines 67-89 (POST) check:
```typescript
const incomingProjects = incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
```

But lines 43-65 (PUT) check:
```typescript
const incomingProjects = incoming?.projects?.length || 0
```

And `session-store.ts` line 214 expects:
```typescript
saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace)
```

**Issue:** The POST handler at line 84 calls `sessionStore.saveWorkspace(req.body)` but checks `incoming?.workspace?.projects`. If the client sends `{ workspace: { projects: [...] } }`, the code will:
1. Check correctly: `incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length` ✓
2. Pass the entire `req.body` (with the wrapper) to saveWorkspace
3. Set `this.data.workspace = { workspace: { projects: [...] } }` ← **Double-wrapped!**

Then on next load, the workspace structure is corrupted.

**Fix needed:** Line 84 should be:
```typescript
sessionStore.saveWorkspace(req.body.workspace || req.body)
```

Or better, unwrap explicitly:
```typescript
sessionStore.saveWorkspace(incoming.workspace || incoming)
```

---

### 3. **middleware.ts** - CORRECT

The CORS hostname validation (lines 34-45) is properly implemented. It correctly:
- Parses the origin URL with `new URL(origin).hostname`
- Extracts just the hostname (not the full URL string)
- Uses safe regex patterns for IP ranges
- Handles MagicDNS (.ts.net) correctly

No issues found.

---

### 4. **orchestrator-api.ts** - CORRECT

Lines 56-78 implement recursive port retry correctly:
- `MAX_PORT_RETRIES` limit prevents infinite loops (line 5, used at line 58)
- `once('error')` (line 70) ensures error handler fires only once per listen attempt
- Retry logic increments port and calls `tryListen(port + 1)` recursively
- Proper guard at line 58: `if (retryCount >= MAX_PORT_RETRIES)` stops recursion

The implementation is safe.

---

### 5. **kspec-adapter.ts** - CRITICAL MULTI-INSTANCE BUG

**Problem:** Module-level state is shared across ALL adapter instances.

Lines 28-30:
```typescript
let _daemonEnsuredFlag = true
let _daemonEnsuredForCwd: string | null = null
```

These are **module-level variables**, not instance properties. If your app creates multiple KspecAdapter instances (e.g., for different projects or concurrent operations):

1. Instance A checks daemon for project `/home/user/ProjectA`
2. Instance B checks daemon for project `/home/user/ProjectB`
3. Instance B sets `_daemonEnsuredForCwd = '/home/user/ProjectB'`
4. Instance A's `list()` call (lines 129-143) sees `_daemonEnsuredForCwd !== cwd` and resets the flag
5. **Instance B now thinks daemon needs re-checking even though it just verified it!**

This causes unnecessary duplicate daemon startup attempts and potential race conditions.

**Fix needed:** Convert to instance properties:
```typescript
export class KspecAdapter implements TaskAdapter {
  private _daemonEnsuredFlag = true
  private _daemonEnsuredForCwd: string | null = null
  
  private async ensureDaemon(cwd: string): Promise<boolean> {
    if (this._daemonEnsuredForCwd !== null && this._daemonEnsuredForCwd !== cwd) {
      this._daemonEnsuredFlag = false
    }
    // ... rest of code with this._daemonEnsuredFlag instead of module-level _daemonEnsuredFlag
  }
}
```

---

### 6. **websocket-manager.ts** - CORRECT

The broadcastPtyExit function (lines 318-342) properly cleans up:
- Deletes from `ptyStreams` (line 340)
- Deletes from `terminalSubscriptions` (line 341)
- Closes all associated WebSocket connections (line 334)

The cleanup in the ws.on('close') handler (lines 174-180) is also correct.

No issues found.

---

### 7. **pty.ts (ipc-handlers/pty.ts)** - CORRECT

The race condition guard at lines 83-85:
```typescript
if (ptyManager.getProcess(id)) {
  ptyManager.write(id, pending.prompt + '\n')
  pending.resolve({ success: true, message: 'Prompt sent to new terminal', sessionCreated: true })
} else {
  pending.resolve({ success: false, error: 'Terminal exited before prompt could be sent' })
}
```

This is correct - it checks if the PTY still exists before writing.

The immediate error resolution on spawn failure (lines 94-102) is also proper.

No issues found.

---

### 8. **meta-project-sync.ts** - CORRECT

The broken symlink cleanup (lines 46-57) properly:
- Checks if path exists with `existsSync`
- Verifies it's a symlink with `stat.isSymbolicLink()` before unlinking
- Handles errors gracefully
- The function is called when symlinks shouldn't exist (line 239)

No issues found.

---

### 9. **auth-rate-limit.ts** - POTENTIAL ISSUE

Line 37 has subtle logic issue:
```typescript
if ((entry.blockedUntil && entry.blockedUntil <= now) || now - entry.lastAttempt > RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS) {
  rateLimitStore.delete(ip)
  return { allowed: true }
}
```

The logic is **correct but could be misleading**. It reads as:
- "If block expired OR window expired, clear and allow"

However, there's a potential timing issue: If an IP is blocked until time T, but we don't see them again until time T + 30 minutes, the window check will be true. The code will delete the entry and allow them. This is correct behavior - after the block expires plus window expires, give them fresh attempts.

But actually, re-reading the logic: if `blockedUntil > now`, the check at line 29 already returns early with "denied". So line 37 only executes if `blockedUntil <= now` (block expired) OR the window has expired entirely.

This is **correct** - the behavior is: once unblocked, if you haven't tried again within the window, reset your attempt counter.

No issues found.

---

### 10. **useBeadsTasks.ts (cycleStatus)** - CORRECT

Lines 320-350 show optimistic update pattern:
- Saves previous state (line 331)
- Updates state immediately (lines 332-334)
- Calls adapter method (line 339)
- Reverts on failure (lines 341-343, 346-347)

The `currentStatus` parameter is passed correctly to `adapter.cycleStatus()`. No issues found.

---

## Summary

| File | Status | Issue |
|------|--------|-------|
| session-store.ts | ❌ Bug | Missing `.tmp` file cleanup in error case |
| workspace.ts | ❌ Bug | POST handler double-wraps workspace object |
| middleware.ts | ✅ OK | CORS hostname parsing is correct |
| orchestrator-api.ts | ✅ OK | Port retry with limit is safe |
| kspec-adapter.ts | ❌ Critical | Module-level state shared across instances |
| websocket-manager.ts | ✅ OK | Cleanup logic is correct |
| pty.ts | ✅ OK | Race condition guard present |
| meta-project-sync.ts | ✅ OK | Symlink cleanup is safe |
| auth-rate-limit.ts | ✅ OK | Logic is correct |
| useBeadsTasks.ts | ✅ OK | Optimistic update pattern correct |

**Priority fixes needed:**
1. **HIGH:** workspace.ts double-wrap bug (data corruption)
2. **HIGH:** kspec-adapter.ts multi-instance bug (state pollution)
3. **MEDIUM:** session-store.ts temp file cleanup (resource leak)

## Code Review: fix/bug-persistence Branch - Analysis Report

I've completed a thorough review of all the specified files in the fix/bug-persistence branch. Here are my findings:

### 1. **session-store.ts** - ISSUE FOUND

**Problem:** Missing temp file cleanup on failure.

Lines 189-201 show atomic write with temp+rename pattern:
```typescript
const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
JSON.parse(written)
renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)
```

**Issue:** If `JSON.parse(written)` or `renameSync` throws, the temp file is never deleted. The catch block at line 198 logs the error but doesn't clean up the `.tmp` file. This will:
- Accumulate stale `.tmp` files on repeated failures
- Potentially cause issues on filesystems with space constraints
- Leave evidence of failed writes

**Fix needed:** Add cleanup in the catch block:
```typescript
} catch (e) {
  try {
    if (existsSync(tmpPath)) unlinkSync(tmpPath)
  } catch { /* ignore cleanup errors */ }
  console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to save workspace:', e)
}
```

---

### 2. **workspace.ts routes** - LOGIC BUG FOUND

**Problem:** POST handler expects wrapped workspace, but GET/PUT handlers and saveWorkspace don't match.

Lines 67-89 (POST) check:
```typescript
const incomingProjects = incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
```

But lines 43-65 (PUT) check:
```typescript
const incomingProjects = incoming?.projects?.length || 0
```

And `session-store.ts` line 214 expects:
```typescript
saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace)
```

**Issue:** The POST handler at line 84 calls `sessionStore.saveWorkspace(req.body)` but checks `incoming?.workspace?.projects`. If the client sends `{ workspace: { projects: [...] } }`, the code will:
1. Check correctly: `incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length` ✓
2. Pass the entire `req.body` (with the wrapper) to saveWorkspace
3. Set `this.data.workspace = { workspace: { projects: [...] } }` ← **Double-wrapped!**

Then on next load, the workspace structure is corrupted.

**Fix needed:** Line 84 should be:
```typescript
sessionStore.saveWorkspace(req.body.workspace || req.body)
```

Or better, unwrap explicitly:
```typescript
sessionStore.saveWorkspace(incoming.workspace || incoming)
```

---

### 3. **middleware.ts** - CORRECT

The CORS hostname validation (lines 34-45) is properly implemented. It correctly:
- Parses the origin URL with `new URL(origin).hostname`
- Extracts just the hostname (not the full URL string)
- Uses safe regex patterns for IP ranges
- Handles MagicDNS (.ts.net) correctly

No issues found.

---

### 4. **orchestrator-api.ts** - CORRECT

Lines 56-78 implement recursive port retry correctly:
- `MAX_PORT_RETRIES` limit prevents infinite loops (line 5, used at line 58)
- `once('error')` (line 70) ensures error handler fires only once per listen attempt
- Retry logic increments port and calls `tryListen(port + 1)` recursively
- Proper guard at line 58: `if (retryCount >= MAX_PORT_RETRIES)` stops recursion

The implementation is safe.

---

### 5. **kspec-adapter.ts** - CRITICAL MULTI-INSTANCE BUG

**Problem:** Module-level state is shared across ALL adapter instances.

Lines 28-30:
```typescript
let _daemonEnsuredFlag = true
let _daemonEnsuredForCwd: string | null = null
```

These are **module-level variables**, not instance properties. If your app creates multiple KspecAdapter instances (e.g., for different projects or concurrent operations):

1. Instance A checks daemon for project `/home/user/ProjectA`
2. Instance B checks daemon for project `/home/user/ProjectB`
3. Instance B sets `_daemonEnsuredForCwd = '/home/user/ProjectB'`
4. Instance A's `list()` call (lines 129-143) sees `_daemonEnsuredForCwd !== cwd` and resets the flag
5. **Instance B now thinks daemon needs re-checking even though it just verified it!**

This causes unnecessary duplicate daemon startup attempts and potential race conditions.

**Fix needed:** Convert to instance properties:
```typescript
export class KspecAdapter implements TaskAdapter {
  private _daemonEnsuredFlag = true
  private _daemonEnsuredForCwd: string | null = null
  
  private async ensureDaemon(cwd: string): Promise<boolean> {
    if (this._daemonEnsuredForCwd !== null && this._daemonEnsuredForCwd !== cwd) {
      this._daemonEnsuredFlag = false
    }
    // ... rest of code with this._daemonEnsuredFlag instead of module-level _daemonEnsuredFlag
  }
}
```

---

### 6. **websocket-manager.ts** - CORRECT

The broadcastPtyExit function (lines 318-342) properly cleans up:
- Deletes from `ptyStreams` (line 340)
- Deletes from `terminalSubscriptions` (line 341)
- Closes all associated WebSocket connections (line 334)

The cleanup in the ws.on('close') handler (lines 174-180) is also correct.

No issues found.

---

### 7. **pty.ts (ipc-handlers/pty.ts)** - CORRECT

The race condition guard at lines 83-85:
```typescript
if (ptyManager.getProcess(id)) {
  ptyManager.write(id, pending.prompt + '\n')
  pending.resolve({ success: true, message: 'Prompt sent to new terminal', sessionCreated: true })
} else {
  pending.resolve({ success: false, error: 'Terminal exited before prompt could be sent' })
}
```

This is correct - it checks if the PTY still exists before writing.

The immediate error resolution on spawn failure (lines 94-102) is also proper.

No issues found.

---

### 8. **meta-project-sync.ts** - CORRECT

The broken symlink cleanup (lines 46-57) properly:
- Checks if path exists with `existsSync`
- Verifies it's a symlink with `stat.isSymbolicLink()` before unlinking
- Handles errors gracefully
- The function is called when symlinks shouldn't exist (line 239)

No issues found.

---

### 9. **auth-rate-limit.ts** - POTENTIAL ISSUE

Line 37 has subtle logic issue:
```typescript
if ((entry.blockedUntil && entry.blockedUntil <= now) || now - entry.lastAttempt > RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS) {
  rateLimitStore.delete(ip)
  return { allowed: true }
}
```

The logic is **correct but could be misleading**. It reads as:
- "If block expired OR window expired, clear and allow"

However, there's a potential timing issue: If an IP is blocked until time T, but we don't see them again until time T + 30 minutes, the window check will be true. The code will delete the entry and allow them. This is correct behavior - after the block expires plus window expires, give them fresh attempts.

But actually, re-reading the logic: if `blockedUntil > now`, the check at line 29 already returns early with "denied". So line 37 only executes if `blockedUntil <= now` (block expired) OR the window has expired entirely.

This is **correct** - the behavior is: once unblocked, if you haven't tried again within the window, reset your attempt counter.

No issues found.

---

### 10. **useBeadsTasks.ts (cycleStatus)** - CORRECT

Lines 320-350 show optimistic update pattern:
- Saves previous state (line 331)
- Updates state immediately (lines 332-334)
- Calls adapter method (line 339)
- Reverts on failure (lines 341-343, 346-347)

The `currentStatus` parameter is passed correctly to `adapter.cycleStatus()`. No issues found.

---

## Summary

| File | Status | Issue |
|------|--------|-------|
| session-store.ts | ❌ Bug | Missing `.tmp` file cleanup in error case |
| workspace.ts | ❌ Bug | POST handler double-wraps workspace object |
| middleware.ts | ✅ OK | CORS hostname parsing is correct |
| orchestrator-api.ts | ✅ OK | Port retry with limit is safe |
| kspec-adapter.ts | ❌ Critical | Module-level state shared across instances |
| websocket-manager.ts | ✅ OK | Cleanup logic is correct |
| pty.ts | ✅ OK | Race condition guard present |
| meta-project-sync.ts | ✅ OK | Symlink cleanup is safe |
| auth-rate-limit.ts | ✅ OK | Logic is correct |
| useBeadsTasks.ts | ✅ OK | Optimistic update pattern correct |

**Priority fixes needed:**
1. **HIGH:** workspace.ts double-wrap bug (data corruption)
2. **HIGH:** kspec-adapter.ts multi-instance bug (state pollution)
3. **MEDIUM:** session-store.ts temp file cleanup (resource leak)
